trim_reasons: VOLATILE tag on a tough defensive matchup with a FG_MARGIN_THIN that forced a mandatory step-down; a single cold shooting night leaves only 5 pts of buffer at T10, and the min_floor of 24.7 means a garbage-time DNP or foul trouble produces a miss with no cushion.

An -8.5 blowout spread creates genuine Q4 rest risk for Jokic's counting stats; assists require sustained offensive possessions in a competitive game, and a Portland team that falls apart early shrinks Jokic's 4th-quarter distribution opportunities precisely when he needs them to clear 8.

VOLATILE tag combined with an -8.5 blowout spread is a compounding risk: if DEN builds a large lead early, Jokic sits in Q4 and REB accumulation halts; the VOLATILE distribution means he can miss badly even in a full-minutes game, and blowout rest is an additional minutes ceiling the iron_floor doesn't protect against.

VOLATILE tag plus a min_floor of only 22.9 on a BLOWOUT_RISK game (-8.5 SA favorite) is the fragile combination: if SA goes up big early, Castle's minutes compress exactly to or below his floor, and the FG_MARGIN_NEG step-down signals his scoring efficiency is already shaky at higher tiers.

B2B with insufficient B2B hit-rate sample means the rest penalty is unquantified, not absent; combined with a -14.5 spread blowout risk and FG_MARGIN_THIN that already forced a step-down, a Q4 early exit could compress Harden's counting stats before he reaches even the conservative T10 threshold.

VOLATILE scorer on B2B with a confirmed 75% B2B hit rate at T20 specifically — materially below his overall rate — facing a -14.5 blowout spread; the FG_MARGIN_THIN step-down already signals a thin scoring cushion, and the compounding of B2B fatigue, blowout rest risk, and volatile distribution creates a structural floor that is genuinely fragile at the T20 threshold.
